---
title: "Post-Lasso"
format: pdf
---

```{r include=FALSE}


library(tidyverse)
library(arrow)
library(glmnet)
library(broom)
library(gt)
library(ggthemes)
library(patchwork)

data <- read_parquet("../data/clean_data.parquet")

```

```{r}
# Build a valid formula from TERM labels
make_formula_from_terms <- function(response, terms_vec) {
  rhs <- paste(terms_vec, collapse = " + ")
  as.formula(paste(response, "~", rhs))
}

# Raw nonzero glmnet coefficient names
# These are model.matrix column names, not formula terms
extract_selected_columns <- function(glmnet_fit) {
  coef(glmnet_fit) %>%
    as.matrix() %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("term") %>%
    dplyr::rename(estimate = s0) %>%
    dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)", estimate != 0) %>%
    dplyr::pull(term)
}

# Convert selected model.matrix columns back to original TERM labels
# like "education" or "education:race_simp"
get_selected_terms <- function(design_formula, data, glmnet_fit) {
  mm <- model.matrix(design_formula, data = data)
  term_labels <- attr(terms(design_formula), "term.labels")
  assign_vec <- attr(mm, "assign")

  selected_cols <- extract_selected_columns(glmnet_fit)

  selected_idx <- match(selected_cols, colnames(mm))
  selected_idx <- selected_idx[!is.na(selected_idx)]

  selected_term_ids <- unique(assign_vec[selected_idx])
  selected_term_ids <- selected_term_ids[selected_term_ids != 0]

  term_labels[selected_term_ids]
}

# Optional helper for prettier table labels
clean_term_names <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    str_remove_all("education") %>%
    str_remove_all("race_simp") %>%
    str_remove_all("cit_simp")
}
```

```{r}

model_inputs <- data %>% 
  dplyr::filter(
    age > 18,
    esr != "Not in Labor Force"
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(
    cit_simp = ifelse(cit != "Not a U.S. citizen", "Citizen", "Non-Citizen"),
    cit_simp = factor(cit_simp, levels = c("Citizen", "Non-Citizen")),
    education = factor(
      education,
      levels = c(
        "Post-bacc professional degree",
        "Doctorate degree",
        "Master's degree",
        "Bachelor's degree",
        "Associate's degree",
        "Some college",
        "Regular high school diploma",
        "GED or alternative credential",
        "Less than High School"
      )
    )
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::select(
    income_personal, race_simp, cit_simp, education, pwgtp, pwgtp1:pwgtp80, age
  )
```

```{r}

lasso_data <- model_inputs %>% 
  mutate(
    log_income = log(income_personal),
    education = relevel(factor(education), ref = "Bachelor's degree"),
    race_simp = relevel(factor(race_simp), ref = "Hispanic"),
    cit_simp  = relevel(factor(cit_simp), ref = "Citizen")
  ) %>% 
  filter(
    is.finite(log_income),
    !is.na(age),
    !is.na(education),
    !is.na(race_simp),
    !is.na(cit_simp),
    !is.na(pwgtp)
  )
```

```{r}
# Save the original TERM-level Lasso formula
lasso_formula_pop <- log_income ~ age + education + race_simp + cit_simp +
  education:race_simp + education:cit_simp + race_simp:cit_simp
```


```{r}
# Create design matrix for Lasso
x_pop <- model.matrix(
  lasso_formula_pop,
  data = lasso_data
)[, -1]

# Response and weights
y_pop <- lasso_data$log_income
w_pop <- lasso_data$pwgtp
```

```{r}
set.seed(544)

cv_lasso_pop <- cv.glmnet(
  x = x_pop,
  y = y_pop,
  alpha = 1,
  family = "gaussian",
  weights = w_pop,
  standardize = TRUE,
  nfolds = 10
)

# Fit the selected Lasso model at lambda.1se
lasso_pop_1se <- glmnet(
  x = x_pop,
  y = y_pop,
  alpha = 1,
  family = "gaussian",
  weights = w_pop,
  standardize = TRUE,
  lambda = cv_lasso_pop$lambda.1se
)
```

```{r}
# raw selected COLUMN names from model.matrix
selected_cols_pop <- extract_selected_columns(lasso_pop_1se)
selected_cols_pop
```


```{r}
# TERM-level selections
selected_terms_pop <- get_selected_terms(
  design_formula = lasso_formula_pop,
  data = lasso_data,
  glmnet_fit = lasso_pop_1se
)

selected_terms_pop
```

```{r}
# Convert selected Lasso terms into the svyglm refit formula
post_lasso_formula_pop <- make_formula_from_terms(
  response = "log_income",
  terms_vec = selected_terms_pop
)

post_lasso_formula_pop
```

```{r}
rep_weights <- paste0("pwgtp", 1:80)

pop_design <- lasso_data %>% 
  as_survey_rep(
    weights = pwgtp,
    repweights = all_of(rep_weights),
    type = "successive-difference",
    mse = TRUE
  )
```

```{r}
# Refit the Lasso-selected model using survey-weighted regression
# This gives design-based standard errors, CIs, and p-values.
post_lasso_pop <- pop_design %>% 
  svyglm(
    formula = post_lasso_formula_pop,
    design = .,
    na.action = na.omit
  )
```

```{r}
# post-Lasso population results
post_lasso_pop_full <- tidy(post_lasso_pop, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    term = clean_term_names(term),
    percent_change = round((exp(estimate) - 1) * 100, 1),
    conf_low = round((exp(conf.low) - 1) * 100, 1),
    conf_high = round((exp(conf.high) - 1) * 100, 1),
    estimate = round(estimate, 3),
    std.error = round(std.error, 3),
    statistic = round(statistic, 3),
    p.value = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "< .001", round(p.value, 3))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(term, percent_change, conf_low, conf_high, statistic, p.value) %>%
  arrange(desc(percent_change))
```

```{r}
# Full table of all post-Lasso refit terms for population
post_lasso_pop_full %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Table: Post-Lasso svyglm Results for Population") %>%
  cols_label(
    term = "Term",
    percent_change = "% Change in Income",
    conf_low = "95% CI Low",
    conf_high = "95% CI High",
    statistic = "t",
    p.value = "p"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note("Lasso selected terms at lambda.1se, then refit with survey-weighted svyglm using ACS SDR replicate weights.")
```

```{r}
# Top 5 increases for population post-Lasso refit
top5_increase_pop <- post_lasso_pop_full %>%
  arrange(desc(percent_change)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Top 5 Largest Income Increases: Population Post-Lasso svyglm") %>%
  cols_label(
    term = "Term",
    percent_change = "% Change in Income",
    conf_low = "95% CI Low",
    conf_high = "95% CI High",
    statistic = "t",
    p.value = "p"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note("Terms selected by Lasso at lambda.1se, then refit with svyglm.")

# Top 5 decreases for population post-Lasso refit
top5_decrease_pop <- post_lasso_pop_full %>%
  arrange(percent_change) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Top 5 Largest Income Decreases: Population Post-Lasso svyglm") %>%
  cols_label(
    term = "Term",
    percent_change = "% Change in Income",
    conf_low = "95% CI Low",
    conf_high = "95% CI High",
    statistic = "t",
    p.value = "p"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note("Terms selected by Lasso at lambda.1se, then refit with svyglm.")

top5_increase_pop
top5_decrease_pop
```

HISPANIC ONLY

```{r}
# Build Hispanic-only analysis dataset following your earlier model structure
model_inputs_hisp <- data %>% 
  dplyr::filter(
    age > 18,
    esr != "Not in Labor Force"
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(
    cit_simp = ifelse(cit != "Not a U.S. citizen", "Citizen", "Non-Citizen"),
    cit_simp = factor(cit_simp, levels = c("Citizen", "Non-Citizen")),
    education = factor(
      education,
      levels = c(
        "Post-bacc professional degree",
        "Doctorate degree",
        "Master's degree",
        "Bachelor's degree",
        "Associate's degree",
        "Some college",
        "Regular high school diploma",
        "GED or alternative credential",
        "Less than High School"
      )
    )
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::filter(race_simp == "Hispanic") %>% 
  dplyr::select(
    income_personal, race_simp, cit_simp, education, pwgtp, pwgtp1:pwgtp80, age
  )
```

```{r}
# Prepare Hispanic-only modeling dataset
lasso_data_hisp <- model_inputs_hisp %>% 
  mutate(
    log_income = log(income_personal),
    education = relevel(factor(education), ref = "Regular high school diploma"),
    cit_simp  = relevel(factor(cit_simp), ref = "Non-Citizen")
  ) %>% 
  filter(
    is.finite(log_income),
    !is.na(age),
    !is.na(education),
    !is.na(cit_simp),
    !is.na(pwgtp)
  )
```

```{r}
# TERM-level Hispanic-only Lasso formula
lasso_formula_hisp <- log_income ~ age + education + cit_simp + education:cit_simp
```


```{r}
# Create design matrix for Hispanic-only Lasso
x_hisp <- model.matrix(
  lasso_formula_hisp,
  data = lasso_data_hisp
)[, -1]

# Response and weights
y_hisp <- lasso_data_hisp$log_income
w_hisp <- lasso_data_hisp$pwgtp
```

```{r}
# Fit cross-validated Hispanic-only Lasso
set.seed(544)

cv_lasso_hisp <- cv.glmnet(
  x = x_hisp,
  y = y_hisp,
  alpha = 1,
  family = "gaussian",
  weights = w_hisp,
  standardize = TRUE,
  nfolds = 10
)

# Fit selected Hispanic-only Lasso model at lambda.1se
lasso_hisp_1se <- glmnet(
  x = x_hisp,
  y = y_hisp,
  alpha = 1,
  family = "gaussian",
  weights = w_hisp,
  standardize = TRUE,
  lambda = cv_lasso_hisp$lambda.1se
)
```

```{r}
# raw selected COLUMN names from model.matrix
selected_cols_hisp <- extract_selected_columns(lasso_hisp_1se)
selected_cols_hisp
```


```{r}
# Extract the exact terms selected by Hispanic-only Lasso at lambda.1se
selected_terms_hisp <- get_selected_terms(
  design_formula = lasso_formula_hisp,
  data = lasso_data_hisp,
  glmnet_fit = lasso_hisp_1se
)

selected_terms_hisp
```

```{r}
# Convert selected Lasso terms into the svyglm refit formula
post_lasso_formula_hisp <- make_formula_from_terms(
  response = "log_income",
  terms_vec = selected_terms_hisp
)

post_lasso_formula_hisp
```

```{r}
# Build Hispanic-only replicate-weight survey design
hisp_design <- lasso_data_hisp %>% 
  as_survey_rep(
    weights = pwgtp,
    repweights = all_of(rep_weights),
    type = "successive-difference",
    mse = TRUE
  )
```

```{r}
# Refit Hispanic-only selected model with survey-weighted regression
post_lasso_hisp <- hisp_design %>% 
  svyglm(
    formula = post_lasso_formula_hisp,
    design = .,
    na.action = na.omit
  )
```

```{r}
# full post-Lasso Hispanic-only results
post_lasso_hisp_full <- tidy(post_lasso_hisp, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    term = clean_term_names(term),
    percent_change = round((exp(estimate) - 1) * 100, 1),
    conf_low = round((exp(conf.low) - 1) * 100, 1),
    conf_high = round((exp(conf.high) - 1) * 100, 1),
    estimate = round(estimate, 3),
    std.error = round(std.error, 3),
    statistic = round(statistic, 3),
    p.value = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "< .001", round(p.value, 3))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(term, percent_change, conf_low, conf_high, statistic, p.value) %>%
  arrange(desc(percent_change))
```

```{r}
# Full table of all post-Lasso refit terms for Hispanic-only model
post_lasso_hisp_full %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Table: Post-Lasso svyglm Results for Hispanic-only Population") %>%
  cols_label(
    term = "Term",
    percent_change = "% Change in Income",
    conf_low = "95% CI Low",
    conf_high = "95% CI High",
    statistic = "t",
    p.value = "p"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note("Lasso selected terms at lambda.1se, then refit with survey-weighted svyglm using ACS SDR replicate weights.")
```

```{r}
# Top 5 increases for Hispanic-only post-Lasso refit
top5_increase_hisp <- post_lasso_hisp_full %>%
  arrange(desc(percent_change)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Top 5 Largest Income Increases: Hispanic-only Post-Lasso svyglm") %>%
  cols_label(
    term = "Term",
    percent_change = "% Change in Income",
    conf_low = "95% CI Low",
    conf_high = "95% CI High",
    statistic = "t",
    p.value = "p"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note("Terms selected by Lasso at lambda.1se, then refit with svyglm.")

# Top 5 decreases for Hispanic-only post-Lasso refit
top5_decrease_hisp <- post_lasso_hisp_full %>%
  arrange(percent_change) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Top 5 Largest Income Decreases: Hispanic-only Post-Lasso svyglm") %>%
  cols_label(
    term = "Term",
    percent_change = "% Change in Income",
    conf_low = "95% CI Low",
    conf_high = "95% CI High",
    statistic = "t",
    p.value = "p"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note("Terms selected by Lasso at lambda.1se, then refit with svyglm.")

top5_increase_hisp
top5_decrease_hisp
```
